# Dave Mullen for Yakima County Commissioner
# Republican Candidate · District 1 · General Election November 3, 2026

> Dave Mullen is a U.S. Air Force veteran and proven executive leader running as the Republican candidate for Yakima County Commissioner, District 1 in Washington State. He brings more than 15 years of executive experience in municipal administration, healthcare operations, and community development.

## Campaign

- Candidate: Dave Mullen
- Office: Yakima County Commissioner, District 1
- Party: Republican
- Primary Election: August 4, 2026
- General Election: November 3, 2026
- Website: https://mullenforyakima.com
- Donate: https://mullenforyakima.com/donate
- Contact: daveforyakimaco@gmail.com
- Phone: (267) 879-5387
- Address: 7772 N Wenas Rd, Selah, WA 98942

## Background

Dave Mullen served in the U.S. Air Force from 2009 to 2015 as Non-Commissioned Officer in Charge of Resource Protection at Eglin Air Force Base. He trained more than 100 Airmen with a 100% end-of-course exam pass rate and earned two Air Force Achievement Medals and the Senior Airman Below-the-Zone promotion.

After his service, Dave held the following public positions in the Yakima Valley:
- Parks & Recreation Director, City of Selah
- Elected Member, Moxee City Council
- Business and Value Analyst, Yakima Valley Memorial Hospital (saved $3.1M in one fiscal year)
- Interim City Administrator, Town of Naches

He holds an MBA and a Public Administration Certificate.

## Platform — Top Priorities

1. **Water Rights** — Protecting water allocations for farmers, ranchers, and rural communities across Yakima County.
2. **Agriculture & Economy** — Supporting the farming economy that drives Yakima County's prosperity.
3. **Fiscal Responsibility** — Eliminating government waste and holding the line on taxes.
4. **Public Safety** — Supporting law enforcement and ensuring District 1 communities are safe.
5. **Responsive Government** — A commissioner who answers the phone, shows up, and delivers results.

## Donations

Contributions to Friends of Dave Mullen are regulated by Washington State PDC rules:
- Limit: $1,200 per person, $2,400 per household per election
- Multiple donations are allowed as long as the total does not exceed the per-election cap
- Employer and profession required for donations over $100
- Contributions are not tax deductible
- WA PDC Disclosure: https://www.pdc.wa.gov

## Pages

- https://mullenforyakima.com — Campaign homepage (bio, issues, experience, contact)
- https://mullenforyakima.com/donate — Donation page (Anedot)
